name: Porst 2012 Tadalafil ED Psychosocial Outcomes Extract
population_or_context: PDE5-inhibitor-naive men with erectile dysfunction; mean age 52 years.
sample_size: 215 randomized: tadalafil once daily N=146; placebo N=69.
methods: Randomized placebo-controlled 12-week trial of tadalafil 5 mg once daily, with possible down-titration to 2.5 mg, measuring ED outcomes, treatment satisfaction, SEAR psychosocial outcomes, endothelial measures, and adverse events.
population_profile: Men with erectile dysfunction, mean age 52; indirect for healthy men age 30 to 50.
tadalafil_exposure: Tadalafil 5 mg once daily with possible down-titration to 2.5 mg for 12 weeks.
indication_context: On-label erectile-dysfunction treatment context; used as indirect psychosocial evidence.
study_design: Randomized placebo-controlled parallel trial in PDE5-inhibitor-naive ED patients.
safety_findings: Abstract reports tadalafil was well tolerated and lists back pain, headache, and dyspepsia as adverse events.
directness_to_target_population: Indirect because participants had ED and were older on average than the primary target group.
confounding_and_bias_limits: Psychosocial effects were measured in the context of improved erectile function and treatment satisfaction; not a general mood or well-being trial.
class_transfer_limits: Direct tadalafil evidence; the main transfer limit is not agent identity but ED population, sexual-function mediation, and endpoint specificity.
